2604.04604.title: AI Agents Under EU Law
2604.04604.paper_summary: AI agents - i.e. AI systems that autonomously plan, invoke external tools, and execute multi-step action chains with reduced human involvement - are being deployed at scale across enterprise functions ranging from customer service and recruitment to clinical decision support and critical infrastructure management. The EU AI Act (Regulation 2024/1689) regulates these systems through a risk-based framework, but it does not operate in isolation: providers face simultaneous obligations under the GDPR, the Cyber Resilience Act, the Digital Services Act, the Data Act, the Data Governance Act, sector-specific legislation, the NIS2 Directive, and the revised Product Liability Directive. This paper provides the first systematic regulatory mapping for AI agent providers integrating (a) draft harmonised standards under Standardisation Request M/613 to CEN/CENELEC JTC 21 as of January 2026, (b) the GPAI Code of Practice published in July 2025, (c) the CRA harmonised standards programme under Mandate M/606 accepted in April 2025, and (d) the Digital Omnibus proposals of November 2025. We present a practical taxonomy of nine agent deployment categories mapping concrete actions to regulatory triggers, identify agent-specific compliance challenges in cybersecurity, human oversight, transparency across multi-party action chains, and runtime behavioral drift. We propose a twelve-step compliance architecture and a regulatory trigger mapping connecting agent actions to applicable legislation. We conclude that high-risk agentic systems with untraceable behavioral drift cannot currently satisfy the AI Act's essential requirements, and that the provider's foundational compliance task is an exhaustive inventory of the agent's external actions, data flows, connected systems, and affected persons.
2604.04604.paper_summary_zh: AI 代理 - 即自主規劃、調用外部工具並執行多步驟行動鏈的 AI 系統，並減少人類參與 - 正在大規模部署於企業功能中，涵蓋從客戶服務和招聘到臨床決策支持和關鍵基礎設施管理等領域。歐盟 AI 法案（條例 2024/1689）通過風險基礎框架對這些系統進行監管，但並不是孤立運作：提供者面臨 GDPR、網絡韌性法、數字服務法、數據法、數據治理法、行業特定立法、NIS2 指令以及修訂的產品責任指令下的同時義務。本文提供了針對 AI 代理提供者的首個系統性監管映射，整合了 (a) 截至 2026 年 1 月的 CEN/CENELEC JTC 21 標準化請求 M/613 下的草擬統一標準，(b) 2025 年 7 月發布的 GPAI 行為準則，(c) 2025 年 4 月接受的 M/606 授權下的 CRA 統一標準計劃，以及 (d) 2025 年 11 月的數字綜合提案。我們提出了一個實用的九類代理部署分類法，將具體行動映射到監管觸發點，識別在網絡安全、人類監督、跨多方行動鏈的透明度以及運行時行為漂移方面的代理特定合規挑戰。我們提出了一個十二步合規架構和一個監管觸發映射，將代理行動與適用立法相連接。我們得出結論，高風險的代理系統如果存在無法追蹤的行為漂移，當前無法滿足 AI 法案的基本要求，而提供者的基礎合規任務是對代理的外部行動、數據流、連接系統和受影響人員進行全面清查。
2604.04604.author: Luca Nannini et.al.
2604.04604.authors: Luca Nannini, Adam Leon Smith, Michele Joshua Maggini, Enrico Panai, Sandra Feliciano, Aleksandr Tiulkanov, Elena Maran, James Gealy, Piercosma Bisconti
